title: CELP Lab @ UMass Boston Species Lists & Codes
text: This page contains brief species identifications and codes used for data recording in the field for all species sampled by the Coastal Ecology for a Living Planet lab. Please use it for reference as a rough field ID guide, although it is likely incomplete. We will note which species cannot be identified in the field where needed.

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Any encrusting (calcified), or hardened, pink/red/purple-ish algae growing in patches. Growing synonymous along rocks/hard surfaces. Can be bumpy, textured, or completely flat. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: NOT erect like Corallina officinalis

Extended Description:

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Misjel Decleer via WORMS
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: It forms long fronds that hang down, typically from hard substrate such as boulders. The fronds have a unpaured airbladders, with a new one generating each year. 
Easily confused species and differentiation: Closely related to Fucus species.

Extended Description:

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: 
Easily confused species and differentiation: Can be confused for Sacchrizia dermatodea, which has a square stipe. Saccharina latissima has a round stipe.

Extended Description:

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: 5 teeth on anterior margin of carapace, narrower than cancer crabs. 
Easily confused species and differentiation:

Extended Description: 
WORMS page

title: CELP Lab Species Guide
text:
Photo from Bernard Picton via WORMS
Link to Unknown ID Page: Unknown hydroid.
Other images:  
Distinguishing features: Erect hydroid with very defined pinnate brances. Looks very coarse given the very obvious branching.
